title: Add-Content without -Encoding writes ANSI files
description: Add-Content called without an explicit -Encoding parameter defaults to the system code page (Windows-1252 / ANSI) for new files in PowerShell 5.1. This produces files incompatible with the rest of the project which uses UTF-8 WITH BOM. Previously tracked as SEMI-SIN-006; promoted to a blocking SIN because file encoding inconsistencies cause parse errors in PS 5.1 and silent data corruption with non-ASCII characters.
detection_method: Regex scan for Add-Content calls that do not include -Encoding on the same line
preventionRule: Always append -Encoding UTF8 to every Add-Content call. For Unicode content use [System.IO.File]::AppendAllText with UTF-8 BOM encoding instead.
remedy: Replace Add-Content <path> -Value <val> with Add-Content <path> -Value <val> -Encoding UTF8. For files containing Unicode characters (emoji, box-drawing etc.) use [System.IO.File]::AppendAllText() with System.Text.UTF8Encoding($true) to guarantee the BOM is present.
